draft.introduction: A psalm of unshakeable confidence in God as cosmic refuge. The refrain “the Lord of hosts is with us” shaped both Jewish and Christian liturgical tradition.

translation.text: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore we will not fear though the earth gives way, though the mountains be moved into the heart of the sea. The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress.
translation.original_text: ὁ θεὸς ἡμῶν καταφυγὴ καὶ δύναμις

translator_notes.note: “Be still and know that I am God” (σχολάσατε καὶ γνῶτε) uses σχολάζω, meaning “to have leisure, to be at rest”—the root of the English word “school.”
